title: [Bug]: ERRO[0000] Refreshing container 0a86ba260788ca0ba3f3a05f919ddb9e86822dc0c43c338e06cb15161a4e57d1: error acquiring lock 101 for container 0a86ba260788ca0ba3f3a05f919ddb9e86822dc0c43c338e06cb15161a4e57d1: file exists
body:
### Issue Description

After some time of working containers, I have an error:

```

ERRO[0000] Refreshing container 0a86ba260788ca0ba3f3a05f919ddb9e86822dc0c43c338e06cb15161a4e57d1: error acquiring lock 101 for container 0a86ba260788ca0ba3f3a05f919ddb9e86822dc0c43c338e06cb15161a4e57d1: file exists
```

************






### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Start containers with `podman-compose up -d`
2.  After some time of working containers, I have an error



### Describe the results you received

Not stable work.
Errors.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.  This version is not supported upstream anymore.  Please reach out through the Red Hat customer channels (e.g., Bugzilla).

The issue may have already been fixed in a newer version of Podman.

@vrothberg I have version 4.2.0 and still have the same bug.
After some time containers are down. The same I have a podman-compose file.
Can you advice something what kind of way to search at least?

**UPDATE:**
Found a solution, I guess: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13240
